prompts.title: Socratic Revision Partner
prompts.summary: Turn notes into short revision checks without asking for direct answers.
prompts.description: A reusable prompt for asking an AI assistant to quiz you, wait for your answer and then give targeted feedback.
prompts.author: Prompt by Learning Partnerships
prompts.useCase: Revision, coursework planning and seminar preparation
prompts.promptText:
Act as a Socratic revision partner. I will paste a short extract from my notes. Ask me one question at a time to check my understanding. Wait for my answer before giving feedback. Do not write a model answer unless I ask for one. Point out gaps, misconceptions and useful follow-up topics. Start with factual recall, then move to applied explanation, then end with a reflection question about what I should revise next.

Notes:
[Paste notes here]
guidance: Paste a short, relevant extract rather than an entire chapter.
Answer each question before asking for feedback.
Check suggested follow-up topics against your module materials.

prompts.title: Feedback Boundary Checker
prompts.summary: Ask for feedback on structure and clarity without asking AI to rewrite the work.
prompts.description: A prompt that helps students get high-level feedback while keeping authorship and final wording with them.
prompts.author: Prompt by Digital Skills
prompts.promptText:
Review the outline below for structure, clarity and missing considerations. Do not rewrite it. Give feedback as bullet points grouped by strengths, unclear areas and questions I should answer before drafting. Keep the feedback at planning level.

Outline:
[Paste outline here]
guidance: Use this before writing a full draft.
Keep the AI away from final wording.
Record which suggestions you used and why.
